title: meeting_minutes_filtered
description: Phase 6 (Stage 2 cascade filter). Produced by `spectrum_systems_core.cascade.executor.run_cascade_filter` after the canonical `meeting_minutes` artifact is written. Each item in `filtered_items` is a strict subset of the items in the source `meeting_minutes` artifact's payload — the cascade NEVER invents items, it only drops them. The append-only data lake keeps BOTH artifacts side-by-side: the raw `meeting_minutes` is the producer of record, the filtered artifact is the operator-facing Stage 2 result. `additionalProperties: false` everywhere so an unknown key fails validation fail-closed.

source_artifact_path.description: Path (data-lake-relative or absolute) to the `meeting_minutes` artifact this filtered subset was derived from. Required so a downstream reader can re-locate the producer and so the cascade's audit trail is reconstructable from the artifact alone.

filter_model.description: Model id used by the cascade filter (e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-6`).

filter_prompt_path.description: Path to the cascade filter prompt file (relative to the package, e.g. `workflows/prompts/cascade_filter_sonnet.md`).

filter_prompt_content_hash.description: sha256 of the filter prompt content. Reproducibility anchor.

chunks_with_invalid_filter_response.description: Count of chunks whose Sonnet filter response failed `cascade_filter_response` schema validation. Those chunks pass through ALL items unchanged (conservative failure mode — keeps false positives instead of dropping true positives). An operator monitors this across a corpus to detect filter-prompt drift.

truncation_count.description: Count of turn_aggregate items whose source_turn_ids list exceeded the 10-turn budget and were truncated before being sent to the filter.

filter_started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the cascade pass began.

filter_completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the cascade pass returned.

filtered_items.description: Object carrying the same 22 array keys as the source meeting_minutes payload. Each value is a subset of the source's items (verbatim — the cascade NEVER mutates an item, it only includes or excludes). Empty arrays are valid (drop-all-from-chunk is a real outcome).

extraction_config.description: Passthrough copy of the source meeting_minutes artifact's `payload.provenance.extraction_config` block — captured here so the filtered artifact is self-describing (a reader does not need to load the source artifact to know which prompt / model / chunk hashes produced the candidate items). The block's shape mirrors `meeting_minutes.schema.json::extraction_config` and is loose here (the source schema owns the field-level constraints).
